label: draft-synthesis
text:
{
  "title": "Spec Room",
  "summary": "A two-pass room that turns a rough idea into a concrete first-pass spec.",
  "problem": "Teams often want to build something before the objective, scope, and acceptance criteria are concrete enough to hand off.",
  "goals": [
    "Turn a rough idea into a clear first-pass spec quickly.",
    "Make ambiguity, risks, and open questions explicit.",
    "End with a buildable v1 plan rather than loose brainstorming."
  ],
  "nonGoals": [
    "Replace deep product discovery or architecture review.",
    "Produce a perfect final spec on the first pass."
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The room owner can provide at least a seed objective.",
    "Participants have enough context to pressure-test the idea."
  ],
  "proposal": [
    "Run a draft pass with role-specific prompts for planner, critic, and implementer.",
    "Synthesize the best ideas into a structured draft spec.",
    "Run a critique pass against the draft before producing the final artifact."
  ],
  "acceptanceCriteria": [
    "The draft includes goals, non-goals, risks, and open questions.",
    "The draft proposes a realistic first version instead of vague future work."
  ],
  "implementationPlan": [
    "Create a classic plugin with planner, critic, and implementer roles.",
    "Add draft and critique prompts plus JSON synthesis.",
    "Expose dashboard metrics for progress, contributions, and artifacts."
  ],
  "risks": [
    "Agents may still sound confident when context is thin.",
    "Prompt drift could make outputs less structured over time."
  ],
  "openQuestions": [
    "Should the room eventually save the final spec to disk?",
    "When does it make sense to extract shared text-room helpers?"
  ]
}
